title: Ensure that the etcd pod specification file ownership is set to root:root

input_row.title: Ensure that the etcd pod specification file ownership is set to root:root
input_row.description: Ensure that the /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml file ownership is set to root:root .
input_row.rationale: The etcd pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml controls various parameters that set the behavior of the etcd service in the master node. etcd is a highly-available key-value store which Kubernetes uses for persistent storage of all of its REST API object. You should set its file ownership to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be owned by root:root .
input_row.audit: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the Control Plane node. For example, stat -c %U:%G /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml Verify that the ownership is set to root:root .
input_row.remediation: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the Control Plane node. For example, chown root:root /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml
input_row.default_value: By default, /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml file ownership is set to root:root .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit mentions using a command (`stat -c %U:%G /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml`) to check the ownership of the etcd pod specification file, which indicates that it is a file system property. However, since this file is part of the Kubernetes control plane and its configuration can be managed through Kubernetes APIs and manifests, we can automate the check.
- The rationale emphasizes the importance of the file ownership for maintaining the integrity of the etcd service, which is a standard Kubernetes service property.
- The ownership of files in the control plane can be verified via the Kubernetes API or through the configuration management of the control plane.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_etcd_file_ownership_root_root

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API to check the configuration of the etcd pod by querying the pod specifications in the `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/` directory.
- The specific command to check the ownership of the etcd manifest file can be automated by running a command on the control plane node that checks the file ownership.
- The expected ownership should be `root:root`.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass: Ownership of `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml` is `root:root`.
  - Fail: Ownership of `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml` is not `root:root`.
- Example command to check ownership: `kubectl exec -it <etcd-pod-name> -- stat -c %U:%G /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml` (replace `<etcd-pod-name>` with the actual name of the etcd pod).
